Defines the type literal grammar production.
Defines reflection parsing judgement names.
Parses Type::<T> primary expressions as TypeLiteralExpr.
Defines which types are reflectable, including permission/refinement/modal normalization, type aliases, reflect-attributed declarations, and built-in structural types.
Defines reflection judgement names and TypeLiteralExpr as the compile-time expression form introduced by Type::<T>.
Defines type categories and CategoryOf mapping for reflected types.
Defines reflected field information for permission, refinement, path, alias, and record application types.
Defines reflected variant information for permission, refinement, path, alias, and enum application types.
Defines reflected state information for permission, refinement, modal state, path, alias, and modal application types.
Defines reflected payload kind, payload type/name extraction, and type module path helpers.
Types TypeLiteralExpr as Type when its contained type is well formed.
Requires introspect.category to be valid for any well-formed Type value.
Requires introspect.fields, variants, and states to be valid only for matching reflectable record, enum, and modal types.
Requires reflected fields, enum variants, and modal states to be returned in declaration order.
Requires introspect.implements_form to evaluate the same class-satisfaction judgement used by Phase 3 typing after substituting monomorphized type arguments.
Evaluates a TypeLiteralExpr to a CtType value when the contained type is well formed.
Evaluates introspect.category to the TypeCategory enum value for a CtType.
Evaluates introspect.fields to a compile-time slice of reflected field information.
Evaluates introspect.variants to a compile-time slice of reflected variant information.
Evaluates introspect.states to a compile-time slice of reflected modal state information.
Evaluates introspect.implements_form to a bool matching ordinary class-satisfaction for the type and form.
Evaluates introspect.type_name to the rendered type string.
Evaluates introspect.module_path to the module path text of the type.
Requires reflection to be pure Phase 2 evaluation with immutable results for one CtMachine except for declarations emitted earlier in Phase 2 order.
Requires reflection to contribute only to Phase 2 evaluation and prevents reified Type values and reflection arrays from surviving into Phase 4 unless emitted or literalized.
Requires reflection diagnostics to be defined by Section 22.6.
Defines quote, quote type, quote pattern, quoted content, expression splice, and identifier splice grammar productions.
Defines quote parsing judgement names.
Defines CaptureQuotedTokens over balanced token sequences and preserved splice markers.
Parses raw quote expressions as QuoteNode with unresolved kind and raw quoted tokens.
Parses quote type forms as QuoteNode with Type kind and raw quoted tokens.
Parses quote pattern forms as QuoteNode with Pattern kind and raw quoted tokens.
Defines Ast kinds, Ast node structure, accessors, and AstOf constructor.
Defines quote nodes, quoted bodies, splice nodes, hygiene records, quote site, emit site, and hygienization use.
Defines quote judgement names.
Defines expected AST kind from Ast typed paths.
Defines compile-time literalizable types for splice compatibility.
Defines splice compatibility by quote context and splice source type.
Requires quote forms to be valid only in compile-time contexts.
Defines quote kind resolution from explicit kind, expected AST type, or unique parseable raw quote category.

applies_to: compiler.typecheck, compiler.parser, compiler.comptime, compiler.diagnostics, oracle.expected-diagnostics, oracle.coverage
summary: Requires quoted content to be syntactically valid in the resolved category and rejects unresolved quote kind.
Requires expression and identifier splices to appear only inside quoted token slices and have source types compatible with the surrounding quoted position.

applies_to: compiler.typecheck, compiler.parser, compiler.comptime, compiler.diagnostics, oracle.expected-diagnostics, oracle.coverage
summary: Restricts SpliceIdentNode to identifier-position splices, forbids it in structural identifier positions, requires other quoted positions to use expression splice, and preserves ordinary language meaning of dollar syntax in type position.
Requires string-valued identifier-position splices to be intentionally unhygienic and bind in the emission environment.
Requires emitter emit calls to have TypeEmitter receiver type and Ast::Item or Ast argument type.
Defines parsing of quoted raw token bodies into expression, statement, item, type, and pattern payloads using ordinary parsers extended with splice nodes.
Defines rendering of compile-time splice values into expression, statement, item, type, pattern, and identifier payloads.

Requires HygienizeAst to preserve quote-site captures, prevent hygienic quoted binders from capturing emission-site names except string identifier splices, and derive deterministic fresh marks from quote site, emit site, and input counter.
Requires references inside quoted fragments to hygienic binders introduced by the same fragment to resolve to the renamed binding after emission.
Requires only explicit alias names in using/import to be hygienic binders and preserves unaliased imported names as written.
Evaluates a quote node by resolving quote kind, parsing the quoted body, building the quote payload, and returning a CtAst with hygiene initialized to the current compile-time site.
Requires QuoteBuild to evaluate splice expressions left-to-right, render each splice, substitute into the quoted payload, and return the resulting fragment as Ast payload.
Defines emission order as derive-generated emissions for the current declaration followed by explicit TypeEmitter.emit calls in source order.
Requires quoted and spliced AST fragments to affect lowering only through declarations and expressions present after Phase 2 expansion, with no runtime Ast representation unless explicitly embedded.
Requires quote, splice, and emission diagnostics to be defined by Section 22.6.
Defines derive attribute, derive target list, derive target declaration, derive contract, and derive clause grammar productions.
Defines derive parsing judgement names.
Requires derive attributes to be parsed by the Chapter 9 attribute parser and interpreted by this section.
Parses a derive target declaration with target Type parameter, optional derive contract, and body.
Parses absence of a derive contract option as an empty clause list.
Parses a present derive contract option through derive clause list parsing.
Parses a derive clause list from a first clause and its tail.
Parses a requires derive clause.
Parses an emits derive clause.
Ends derive clause tail parsing when there is no comma.
Continues derive clause tail parsing after a comma by parsing another clause and recursing.
Defines derive target declaration AST form and derive requires/emits contract extractors.

Defines derive requests, annotated declarations, execution judgement names, graph edges, graph construction, stable topological order, target type, visible target lookup, and declared implementation names.
Requires derive attributes to be valid only on record, enum, and modal declarations.

applies_to: compiler.typecheck, compiler.name-resolution, compiler.comptime, compiler.diagnostics, oracle.expected-diagnostics, oracle.coverage
summary: Requires each derive target name in a derive attribute to resolve to exactly one visible derive target declaration at the annotated declaration.
Defines fixed bindings available inside derive target declaration bodies.
Requires derive target bodies to execute under the same restrictions as any other compile-time procedure body.
Requires derive execution order for one annotated type declaration to be the topological order of derive requests with edges from requires/emits intersections.
Requires incomparable derive targets to preserve source order from the derive attribute as the tie-breaker.
Requires every class in DeriveReqs(name) to belong to DeclaredImplNames(D) before executing that derive target for D.
Requires every class in DeriveEmits(name) to belong to DeclaredImplNames(D) before executing that derive target for D.
Requires requires and emits clauses to participate only in derive ordering and explicit implements-list validation and not to add or remove class implementations.
Requires DeriveTargetDecl to be compile-time-only, visible to later derive lookup in Phase 2 module order, and absent from the expanded Phase 3 module set.
Expands a derive target declaration to no runtime items and unchanged compile-time state.
Runs an empty derive target set to no emitted items and unchanged state.
Runs a non-empty derive target set by resolving and running the head derive target, then running the rest and concatenating emitted items.
Runs one derive target by binding derive inputs, executing its body, collecting pending emissions, and clearing the pending-emission queue.
Defines derive target input binding for target type and visible TypeEmitter, Introspect, and ComptimeDiagnostics capabilities.
Expands a derive-annotated declaration by preserving the declaration, running derive targets in derive order, and returning emitted items.
Requires each derive target to execute exactly once per annotated declaration immediately after introducing that declaration and before later source items in the same module.
Requires Phase 2 failure and compilation rejection when a derive target emits diagnostics.error, panics, or emits ill-formed declarations.
Requires derive targets to introduce no runtime dispatch or metadata and affect lowering only through declarations emitted during Phase 2.
Requires derive target and derive contract diagnostics to be defined by Section 22.6.
Defines compile-time execution, reflection, quoting, emission, file-access, and derive diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Requires diagnostics.error, diagnostics.warning, and diagnostics.note to append the specified diagnostic records with the current compile-time site span.
Defines extern calls and foreign invocations of exported procedures as foreign-function boundary crossings.
Defines the FFIBoundary predicate for extern procedure declarations and exported procedure declarations.
Requires FfiSafe to introduce no additional concrete syntax.
Requires FfiSafe to introduce no additional parsing rules.
Defines FfiSafeType as a semantic predicate over existing Type AST forms.
Defines FfiSafeType(T) as compatibility with the platform C ABI runtime representation.
Defines the FfiSafe judgement family.
Defines the primitive types that satisfy the primitive FfiSafe rule.
Defines HasLayoutC and PayloadTypes helper relations used by FfiSafe record and enum rules.
Defines TypeParamSet for FfiSafe generic parameter analysis.
Defines alias parameter and alias predicate-clause lookup helpers for FfiSafe alias application.
Defines TypeSubst and ModalRefSubst equations used by generic FfiSafe rules.
Defines TypeParamsIn and TypeParamsInModalRef equations used by FfiSafe generic-bound analysis.
Defines helper functions for finding type parameters in record fields and enum payloads.
Defines helpers for checking that generic parameters used by FfiSafe types have FfiSafe predicate requirements.
Defines the type categories that are prohibited from satisfying FfiSafeType.
Defines helper predicates that determine when DropType by-value FFI use requires ffi_pass_by_value.
Allows primitive types in FfiPrimTypes to satisfy FfiSafeType.
Allows raw pointer types to satisfy FfiSafeType.
Allows constant-length arrays to satisfy FfiSafeType when their element type is FfiSafe.
Allows function types to satisfy FfiSafeType when every parameter and return type is FfiSafe.
Allows permission-qualified types to satisfy FfiSafeType when their underlying type is FfiSafe.
Allows a type alias path to satisfy FfiSafeType when its alias body is FfiSafe.
Allows a generic type alias application to satisfy FfiSafeType after defaulting, substitution, predicate-clause checking, and substituted alias-body checking.
Allows a non-generic layout(C) record path to satisfy FfiSafeType when layout exists, all fields are FfiSafe, and predicate-clause checks are satisfied.
Allows a generic layout(C) record application to satisfy FfiSafeType after argument defaulting, substitution, predicate-clause checking, and substituted field checking.
Allows a non-generic layout(C) enum path to satisfy FfiSafeType when layout exists, all payload fields are FfiSafe, and predicate-clause checks are satisfied.
Allows a generic layout(C) enum application to satisfy FfiSafeType after argument defaulting, substitution, predicate-clause checking, and substituted payload checking.
Emits the prohibited-category FfiSafe diagnostic when ProhibitedFfiType holds.
Emits the record layout(C) FfiSafe diagnostic when a record type lacks layout(C).
Emits the enum layout(C) FfiSafe diagnostic when an enum type lacks layout(C).
Emits the record-field FfiSafe diagnostic when a layout(C) record path has a non-FfiSafe field.
Emits the record-field FfiSafe diagnostic when a generic layout(C) record application has a substituted non-FfiSafe field.
Emits the enum-field FfiSafe diagnostic when a layout(C) enum path has a non-FfiSafe payload field.
Emits the enum-field FfiSafe diagnostic when a generic layout(C) enum application has a substituted non-FfiSafe payload field.
Emits the incomplete-layout FfiSafe diagnostic when no layout can be computed for a record or enum type.
Emits the generic-unbounded FfiSafe diagnostic for a generic record path with unconstrained field type parameters.
Emits the generic-unbounded FfiSafe diagnostic for a generic enum path with unconstrained payload type parameters.
Emits the generic-unbounded FfiSafe diagnostic for a generic record application with unconstrained field type parameters.
Emits the generic-unbounded FfiSafe diagnostic for a generic enum application with unconstrained payload type parameters.
Requires the listed type categories not to satisfy FfiSafeType.
Requires by-value FFI appearances of types satisfying both DropType and FfiSafeType to carry ffi_pass_by_value on the defining type.
Requires every type parameter used in a field type or variant payload of a FfiSafe type to be bounded by FfiSafe(X) in the declaration predicate clause.
Requires FfiSafe to introduce no additional runtime mechanism and delegates dynamic boundary behavior to extern, export, and unwinding rules.
Requires FfiSafeType to constrain values lowered across FFI boundaries without adding feature-local lowering beyond ABI and boundary rules.
Defines FfiSafe diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Defines the grammar for extern procedure declarations.
Parses an extern procedure declaration into an ExternProcDecl with attributes, visibility, name, generics, predicate clause, signature, contract, foreign contracts, span, and doc fields.
Defines the ExternProcDecl AST tuple fields.
Defines ProcName and ExternRawName derived forms for extern procedure declarations.
Defines an extern procedure as a declaration whose implementation is provided by foreign code.
Defines permitted extern ABI strings, target-profile restrictions, and ExternAbiOk.
Defines extern signature admissibility in terms of return and parameter FfiSafeType checks and FfiByValueOk checks.
Requires extern signatures to reject closure types, accept only sparse function pointer types as FFI-safe, and reject generic type parameters in sparse function pointer types.
Requires calls to extern procedures to appear within an unsafe block.
Requires extern calls to transfer control to foreign code and applies catch or abort unwind behavior at the boundary.
Requires extern lowering to use import-side unwind landing pads from Section 23.7 and adds no other feature-local lowering beyond ABI selection and unsafe-call boundary.
Defines extern procedure diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Assigns FfiSafeType and by-value FFI type-admissibility diagnostics for extern procedures to Section 23.1.7.
Defines a raw exported procedure as a procedure carrying export(abi) and delegates attribute syntax to Section 23.4.1.
Requires raw exported procedures to be parsed by the ordinary procedure-declaration parser.
Requires ordinary ProcedureDecl items to be classified as raw exported procedures when their attributes include export(abi) parsed by Section 23.4.2.
Defines raw exported procedures as ordinary ProcedureDecl items with ExportAttr defined and no dedicated AST node.
Defines a raw exported procedure as a Ultraviolet procedure made callable from foreign code through export.
Defines ZeroBits, ZeroValue, and ZeroableType used by exported procedure catch-return requirements.
Defines ExportSigOk judgement family and export parameter type extraction.
Accepts a raw exported procedure signature when it is public, uses a supported ABI, has FfiSafe return and parameter types, satisfies by-value rules, and catch mode has a zeroable return type.
Requires raw exported procedure bodies to follow ordinary procedure semantics, boundary panic handling to follow Section 23.7, and catch mode to return ZeroValue(R).
Requires raw export calls for RawExportLibrary projects to establish, reuse, and destroy exactly one live loaded library image state according to Section 24.4.4.
Requires ordinary Ultraviolet calls across a shared-library link boundary to establish, reuse, and destroy exactly one live loaded library image state according to Section 24.4.4.
Requires raw export lowering to use export-side unwind frames from Section 23.7 and adds no other feature-local lowering beyond export ABI selection and external linkage.
Defines raw export diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Assigns unsupported export ABI diagnostics to Section 23.2.7 and FfiSafeType/by-value FFI diagnostics to Section 23.1.7.
Defines a hosted export as a procedure carrying host_export(abi) whose foreign-visible signature is derived with an opaque hosted-library session handle.
Requires hosted exports to be parsed by the ordinary procedure-declaration parser.
Requires ordinary ProcedureDecl items to be classified as hosted exports when their attributes include host_export(abi) parsed by Section 23.4.2.
Defines hosted exports as ordinary ProcedureDecl items with HostExportAttr defined.

Defines hosted export classification, context parameter extraction, visible parameter extraction, export sets, hosted-library classification, mixed-mode classification, root capability collection, hosted context bundle constraints, ABI version, session parameter, thunk parameters, foreign parameter types, and thunk signature.
Defines HostedRootCaps as the maximal capability set visible to Ultraviolet user code through hosted exports.
Defines a hosted export as a Ultraviolet procedure made callable from foreign code through a hosted-library session.
Requires hosted-export foreign-visible signatures to consist of a leading usize session-handle parameter followed by source parameters after the first source parameter.
Requires hosted-export foreign-visible pass kind for each visible source parameter to be derived by ForeignABIParam(T_i), independent of source parameter mode.
Defines the HostExportSigOk judgement family.

Accepts a hosted export signature when it is public, non-generic, in a library, not mixed with raw exports, has an explicit hosted context bundle, uses a supported ABI, has FfiSafe visible parameters and return type, carries no capabilities in visible parameters or return type, satisfies by-value rules, and catch mode has a zeroable return type.
Emits the hosted-export library diagnostic when a hosted export is declared outside a library project.
Emits the hosted-export mixed-mode diagnostic when hosted and raw exports are mixed in one hosted library.
Emits the hosted-export generic diagnostic when a host_export procedure has generic parameters.
Emits the hosted-export context diagnostic when a host_export procedure lacks a leading context bundle parameter.
Emits the hosted-export raw-context diagnostic when the leading context parameter is raw Context instead of an explicit projected context bundle.
Emits the hosted-export context move diagnostic when the leading hosted context bundle parameter uses move.
Requires hosted export calls to use a live idle hosted-library session handle of ABI type usize and reject zero as invalid.
Requires hosted-export boundaries to introduce only the reconstructed first source argument as a capability-bearing value, never expose raw Context, and keep visible roots within HostedRootCaps.
Requires successful hosted sessions to grant exactly HostedRootCaps through runtime-owned standard providers and forbids foreign-supplied capability values across the hosted-session ABI.

Requires hosted-export boundaries to validate the session handle, reject invalid states before user code, recover session context, build the first source argument, and invoke the source procedure under ordinary semantics.
Requires hosted-export invalid, non-live, or busy handles to return ZeroValue(R) in catch mode and otherwise abort.
Requires any hosted-export boundary failure before or during invocation in catch mode to return ZeroValue(R).
Requires hosted-export lowering to preserve raw-FFI rules for the foreign-visible signature while reconstructing the first source parameter internally.
Requires hosted-export foreign-visible thunk ABI to be determined by ForeignABICall, ConventionLayout, AssignParamRegs, and the raw-export-equivalent return and stack layout.
Defines hosted thunk carrier judgement families, thunk parameter and return shape helpers, integer lanes, and aggregate LLVM classification.
Lowers a by-reference hosted thunk parameter as a direct const pointer carrier.
Lowers a by-value hosted thunk parameter as a direct LLVM type outside Win64 aggregate special handling.
Lowers a Win64 by-value aggregate hosted thunk parameter of size 1, 2, 4, or 8 as a direct integer lane.
Lowers a Win64 by-value aggregate hosted thunk parameter with unsupported direct integer-lane size as an indirect const pointer carrier.
Lowers a hosted thunk return using LLVMRetLower outside Win64 by-value aggregate special handling.
Lowers a Win64 by-value aggregate hosted thunk return of size 1, 2, 4, or 8 as a direct integer lane without sret.
Lowers a Win64 by-value aggregate hosted thunk return with unsupported direct integer-lane size as an indirect sret return.
Requires hosted-export thunk lowering to use HostThunkParamShape and HostThunkRetShape as the foreign ABI shape.
Forbids splitting one by-value aggregate hosted-export source parameter into multiple scalar ABI parameters at the x86_64-win64 foreign-visible thunk boundary.
Forbids hosted-export-specific ABI rewriting beyond prepending HostSessionAbiParam and omitting the first source parameter.
Requires hosted thunk foreign parameter classification to be mode-independent and pointer-typed visible parameters to use canonical C-style pointer carriers.

Requires thunk-to-source reconstruction to materialize a temporary storage cell and pass it according to ABIParam when foreign ABI parameter classification differs from source ABI parameter classification.

Requires hosted-library thunk and body emission to resolve HostedStateSym loads and stores by full symbol identity and session context and forbids substituting ZeroValue or defaults for failed symbol materialization.

Requires every hosted library to emit the specified foreign-callable lifecycle exports with their names, ABIs, return values, session initialization and destruction behavior, failure cleanup, and handle validity behavior.
Requires hosted lifecycle exports to be backend-generated boundary declarations, not user-declared ProcedureDecl items, and emitted exactly once in each hosted library linked image.

Requires hosted lifecycle exports not to propagate Panic and requires session_destroy failure after accepting a live idle handle to return 0, retire the handle, and reclaim remaining session-private runtime state.
Forbids reissuing a nonzero hosted-session handle token later in the same process lifetime.

Requires every hosted export to emit one foreign-callable thunk selected by LinkName whose ABI prepends a session handle, omits the first source parameter, reconstructs that parameter, rejects invalid handles before user code, and applies raw-export unwind boundary rules.

Requires hosted-export thunks to be backend-generated boundary declarations emitted exactly once per hosted export, use HostThunkLinkName as the foreign symbol, keep Ultraviolet calls targeting Mangle(proc), and forbid exposing Mangle(proc) as the hosted foreign entrypoint.
Defines hosted export diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Assigns FfiSafeType and by-value FFI diagnostics for hosted-export visible parameters and returns to Section 23.1.7.
Defines grammar for mangle, library, unwind, export, host_export, and ffi_pass_by_value attributes.

Requires FFI attributes to be parsed by the general attribute parser, with argument classification, target checking, and attribute-specific constraints applied by the referenced chapters and this section.
Defines FFI attributes as ordinary attribute-list entries attached to their owning declarations.
Defines target kinds for mangle, library, unwind, export, host_export, and ffi_pass_by_value attributes.
Defines mangle attribute validity and link-name selection for none and string modes and rejects non-FFI uses.
Defines library link-kind names and meanings.
Defines library attribute validity, name argument interpretation, default kind, manifest independence, and library-resolution obligation.
Defines platform-specific library name resolution and library-kind support.
Requires declarations whose library kind is unsupported for the selected target profile to be ill-formed.

Requires raw-dylib imports to resolve the Windows DLL and foreign symbol by resolved DLL name and declared symbol name, allows implementation-defined lazy resolution, and does not require PE DELAYLOAD.
Defines unwind modes and their boundary behavior.
Requires abort to be assumed when unwind is not specified.
Requires unwind to be valid only on extern procedure declarations, raw exported procedures, and hosted exports.
Requires catch unwind mode to use C-unwind ABI at extern, raw export, and hosted export boundaries.
Defines export attribute target validity, public visibility requirement, ABI selection, external linkage, link-name selection, and FFI safety requirements.

Defines host_export attribute target validity, public and library requirements, thunk external linkage, ABI selection, link-name selection, hosted-export signature requirements, and prohibition on mixing with export in the same assembly.
Defines ffi_pass_by_value as eligibility for by-value FFI passing of record or enum types satisfying DropType and FfiSafeType, and makes unmarked by-value uses ill-formed.

Defines general FFI attribute constraints, including allowed targets, duplicate symbol diagnostics, unsupported library kinds, redundant-warning cases, host_export library requirement, and host_export/export mixing prohibition.

Requires FFI attributes not to directly evaluate to runtime values and identifies their runtime-relevant effects through unwind behavior, linkage, signature admissibility, hosted-session lowering, and boundary behavior.
Defines lowering effects of mangle, library, export, host_export, unwind, and ffi_pass_by_value attributes.
Defines FFI attribute diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Requires capability isolation to introduce no additional concrete syntax.
Requires capability isolation to introduce no additional parsing rules.
Defines capability-isolation checks as ranging over existing FFI signature types and declarations without a dedicated AST node.

Requires foreign code not to receive or return capability-bearing values, rejects raw and hosted FFI signatures containing Context, capability classes, or dynamic class objects, and forbids region-local raw pointers from crossing FFI boundaries.
Defines helper predicates for region-local provenance, raw pointer types, and FFI call detection.
Rejects an FFI call argument whose provenance is region-local and whose expression type is a raw pointer.
Rejects a return statement from an exported procedure when the returned expression has region-local provenance and raw pointer type.
Requires capability isolation to introduce no runtime mechanism because ill-formed capability-bearing FFI signatures are rejected statically.
Requires capability isolation to add no lowering rules beyond signature-admissibility checks defined by Sections 23.1 through 23.4.
Defines capability-isolation diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Assigns capability-bearing type violations other than region-local raw-pointer escape to the signature and type-admissibility diagnostics owned by Section 23.1.7.
Defines grammar for FFI verification attributes, foreign contract clauses, and ensures predicates.
Defines the token predicate that starts a foreign contract clause.
Parses an absent optional foreign contract clause list when ForeignContractStart does not hold.
Parses a present optional foreign contract clause list when ForeignContractStart holds.
Parses a foreign contract clause list by parsing the first clause and continuing through the tail.
Ends parsing of a foreign contract clause list tail when the next token sequence does not start a foreign contract.
Continues parsing a foreign contract clause list tail by appending the next parsed clause.
Parses a foreign_assumes clause into a ForeignContractClause with ForeignAssumes kind and one predicate.
Parses a foreign_ensures clause into a ForeignContractClause using the parsed ensures predicate kind and expression.
Defines mapping from ensures predicate forms to foreign ensures clause kinds and predicate expressions.
Parses an @error ensures predicate.
Parses an @null_result ensures predicate.
Parses a plain ensures predicate expression.
Defines foreign contract AST attachment and ForeignContractClause, ForeignContractKind, and EnsuresPredicate forms.
Defines the concrete ensures-predicate AST forms.
Defines foreign preconditions as caller obligations specified with foreign_assumes.
Defines what foreign contract predicates may and must not reference.
Defines static and dynamic verification-mode behavior for foreign preconditions.
Requires static foreign preconditions to use StaticProof and dynamic foreign preconditions to insert ContractCheck before the foreign call.
Defines foreign postconditions as successful-return guarantees specified with foreign_ensures.
Defines what foreign postcondition predicates may reference.
Defines SuccessCond, ErrCond, NullCond, foreign call error classification, and postcondition implication obligations.
Requires null_result predicates to appear only when the return type is a nullable pointer type and enumerates nullable pointer forms.
Defines NullableFfiResult and NullResultEnsures helpers for null_result postcondition validation.
Rejects an extern procedure with null_result ensures predicates when the return type is not a nullable FFI result.
Requires error predicates to appear only on foreign procedures whose return type is not unit.
Defines static and dynamic verification-mode behavior for foreign postconditions.
Requires static foreign postconditions to use StaticProof with SuccessCond and ErrCond gating obligations.
Summarizes static and dynamic foreign-contract verification behavior for preconditions and postconditions.
Requires a failed dynamic ForeignPre check to trigger a panic.
Requires dynamic foreign postconditions to evaluate ErrCond and NullCond in left-to-right predicate order, insert ContractCheck after the foreign call, and panic with ContractViolation on failure.
Requires dynamic foreign contracts to lower to ContractCheck before the foreign call for ForeignPre and after the foreign call for ForeignPost, while static contracts introduce no runtime checks.
Defines foreign contract diagnostic and runtime panic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions.
Defines boundary unwinding as owning only unwind syntax from Section 23.4.1 and introducing no additional concrete syntax.
Requires boundary unwinding to introduce no additional parsing rules.
Defines boundary unwind policy as derived from the unwind attribute attached to a procedure declaration.
Defines UnwindModeValue, UnwindMode, default abort behavior, and UnwindAttr extraction.
Defines DetermineUnwindMode, including default abort mode, parsing a single unwind attribute, and duplicate unwind diagnostic emission.
Defines ParseUnwindArg for abort and catch string literals and emits E-SYS-3355 for invalid arguments.
Accepts an unwind mode when the unwind attribute is abort or catch.
Emits E-SYS-3355 when an unwind attribute value is not abort or catch.
Requires abort-mode boundary crossings to abort and catch-mode boundaries to convert imported unwinds to Ultraviolet panics or return ZeroValue for raw and hosted exports.
Assigns general destruction and unwind cleanup semantics to Section 24.5.
Defines code generation effects of abort and catch unwind modes for import and export/hosted-export FFI boundaries.
Emits an abort landing pad for abort-mode extern call sites.
Emits a catch landing pad that converts foreign unwinds to Ultraviolet panics for catch-mode extern call sites.
Emits an abort-on-panic frame for abort-mode exported entry points.
Emits a catch export frame that returns the boundary zero for catch-mode exported entry points.
States that boundary unwinding introduces no additional named diagnostics.
Assigns unwind placement and argument-validation diagnostics to Section 23.4.7.
Defines remaining FFI diagnostic codes, severities, detection phases, and conditions not attached to surface-attribute or foreign-contract subsections.

Defines Chapter 24.1 as owning only the shared lowering interface, correctness obligations, and project/module composition rules, while feature-local lowering rules remain owned by Chapters 12 through 22.
Defines build artifact collection, LLVM IR target, object target, LLVM validity, and the requirement that LowerIR produce valid LLVM 21.1.8 IR.
Defines the shared codegen judgement family.
Defines IRDefined as an IR program having an ExecIRSigma result for every runtime state.
Defines expression, statement, and block codegen correctness predicates relating source semantics to ExecIRSigma behavior.
Defines aggregate codegen correctness, undefined codegen, and classifies undefined codegen as outside conformance.
Defines IR declaration/module forms, LLVM emission judgements, CodegenItem declaration membership, and ProcIR shape.
Defines panic out parameter insertion for codegen parameter lists.
Defines lowered parameter lists for record methods, class methods, substituted class methods, modal state methods, and transitions.
Defines sequential IR composition for zero, one, and multiple IR fragments.
Defines the child-expression left-to-right evaluation order judgement family.

Defines helper functions that extract child expressions from argument lists, field initializers, optional expressions, parallel options, spawn options, dispatch options, key paths, key clauses, and race arms.
Defines left-to-right child expression lists for each expression form that participates in lowering evaluation order.
Defines expression lowering judgement family and RetType helper.
Lowers an expression only when the produced IR preserves EvalSigma behavior under ExecIRSigma.
Defines the core expression forms and the totality obligation for LowerExpr over those forms.
Defines IR execution and move-state judgement families.
Executes ReadVarIR by looking up a variable value and returning it without changing state.
Executes ReadPathIR by looking up a path value and returning it without changing state.
Executes StoreVarIR through WritePlaceSigma on the target identifier.
Executes StoreVarNoDropIR through WritePlaceSubSigma on the target identifier.
Executes BindVarIR by binding a value to a variable and returning ok with the updated state.
Executes ReadPtrIR through ReadPtrSigma.
Executes WritePtrIR through WritePtrSigma.
Defines allocation target selection from the active target or a resolved explicit target.
Executes AllocIR by selecting an allocation target and allocating into that region.
Moves a root place by setting its binding state to Moved.
Moves a field place by applying the field move-state transition and setting the binding state.
Executes MoveStateIR by applying MoveStateSigma and returning ok.
